name: Type Icon Appearance Preference

upstream.requirementSummary: [FE] Let User chose between monochrome and colored icons

sourcePriority: Jira AIQSS-15797 is the upstream requirement source.
Figma references are the visual/source component references.
teletronics-mcp provides product and platform context.
Local app and figma files are the reproducible drawing cache for agents.

options.label: Colorful
options.meaning: Supported themed icons use configured palette colors.

options.label: Monochrome
options.meaning: Supported themed icons use neutral monochrome styling.

requirements: Preference exists in User Preferences Appearance.
Choice is saved per account and survives sign-out/sign-in and session refresh.
Choice applies system-wide to standard UI icons that support theming.
New users or users without a saved value default to Colorful.
Icons remain distinguishable and usable in both modes.
Changing other preferences such as theme or language does not reset icon appearance.

preferencesDialogContent.copyRule: Canonical Preferences dialog. When drawing outside User-profile-settings, copy the whole dialog instance into the target file and clone that copied instance.

steps.action: Open the authored avatar popup dialog near the bottom sidebar avatar, then click Preferences
Open modal preferences dialog on the default Document tab by placing Backdrop behind the copied canonical Preferences dialog
Select Colorful or Monochrome radio option
Optionally show affected TaskItem type/subtype icons as the downstream result example

drawingRequirements: Draw full User Preferences flow frames, not only the dialog.
Start from any app with SUI Sidebar and avatar visible.
Show the authored avatar popup dialog with Preferences item; do not rebuild it from Menu/MenuItem primitives.
Use SUI Backdrop behind the copied canonical Preferences dialog for the modal state.

Open the copied Preferences dialog on Tab=Document by default, then show Appearance tab active in the next state by setting the copied dialog's nested <DialogInners> Preferences instance property Tab=Appearance.
Use RadioGroup and FormControlLabel | Radio for Colorful and Monochrome options.
Show a preview that updates between colorful and monochrome rendering.
Use Task Management type/subtype icons only as the downstream result example.
Use published SUI components and variables; do not modify the design system file.
If the canonical popup or Preferences dialog has not been copied into the target file and its inner components cannot be imported, stop and ask for a copy instead of approximating from primitives.

avatarPopup.copyRule: Copy the whole authored popup dialog node 62:11471 from User-profile-settings into the target file, then duplicate or clone that copied instance into the app frame near the bottom sidebar avatar.
avatarPopup.rule: The copied popup already contains the user menu options. Do not draw those options separately from Menu/MenuItem primitives.

preferencesModal.rule: Copy the whole canonical Preferences dialog into the target file and place it over Backdrop. The default opened modal uses Tab=Document and Linked Entities=Yes; switch to Tab=Appearance only for the later icon appearance state.
